title: Short-Lived Code Exchange to Establish Session on Target Host
description:
The target tenant host must exchange a short-lived OIDC authorization code with Stytch server-to-server to establish a session, setting an httpOnly cookie for subsequent requests.

## Flow

1. Auth domain redirects back to target host with `?code=...` (OIDC authorization code)
2. Target host exchanges code server-to-server with Stytch
3. Receives `session_token` and `session_jwt` from Stytch
4. Sets `session_jwt` in an httpOnly cookie on the target host

## Security Requirements

- Code cannot be replayed — invalid/expired codes fail safely
- Exchange is server-to-server only (code never exposed to client-side)
- Stytch handles all session creation and JWT signing
- No custom session storage or signing logic needed
acceptance_criteria: Target host exchanges OIDC code for session tokens via Stytch SDK
session_jwt is set as an httpOnly secure cookie on the target host
Expired or replayed authorization codes are rejected safely
Code exchange is server-to-server only, never client-side
Session is fully established after code exchange
middleware_changes: OIDC code exchange endpoint on the target host. Uses Stytch SDK to exchange the authorization code for session tokens and sets the session_jwt cookie.
implementation_notes: Stytch SDK handles the code exchange and session creation. The target host only needs to call the SDK and set the resulting JWT as a cookie. No custom cryptographic operations required.
